notes: Mr. D. Dean — the third faction. Per _COMMUNITY_PLANNED_SPEC.md §Dean's network. Dean is not a dispatchable agent, has no BBS boards, has no DMs. He shows up only as anomalies on the map, fingerprints in other people's BBS threads, and tower-brightness events. The engine reads this file to drive the substrate / tower / anomaly system; the player NEVER sees Dean's ledger directly.

tower.label: The Tower
tower.flavor: A single structure in Small Wood, near but not on Frasier's seed-planting nodes. Dean's primary focusing point for substrate manipulation. Visible on Frasier's map but unreachable — agents dispatched to the tower do not return.

brightness_states.label: bright — Dean is doing something this week

agent_dispatch_penalty.log: Agent dispatched to the tower. Tower does not return agents.

substrate_anomaly_palette.title: A seed in Small Wood that should not have lasted
substrate_anomaly_palette.description: A planting attempt that should have been swatted is, this week, still planted. The local press did not run the column they were going to run. The contact is, this week, still picking up.
substrate_anomaly_palette.fingerprint_text: A plain-faced man was seen Sunday at the diner that doesn't know it's a node yet. He ordered coffee and toast. He paid in cash. He left a hundred-dollar bill at the next booth on his way out.

substrate_anomaly_palette.title: Harmony Creek problem resolves overnight
substrate_anomaly_palette.description: A problem Frasier was about to dispatch on is — Tuesday morning — already not a problem. The HOA's motion was tabled. The newsletter ran a different item. The model home, this Tuesday, has the right feel.

engine_effect.log: [i]Anomaly[/i] · the problem at the cul-de-sac resolved itself overnight. Nobody on Frasier's network can account for it.

substrate_anomaly_palette.fingerprint_text: Mackenzie posts on MAINSTREET: 'Anyone else see the lapels guy at the Tuesday meeting? Sat in the back. Didn't speak. Left when the motion was tabled.'

substrate_anomaly_palette.title: A demon that should have corrupted didn't
substrate_anomaly_palette.description: A demon Frasier sent to Small Wood for the kind of work the demon usually corrupts at comes back untouched. Slightly different — but untouched.

engine_effect.log: [i]Anomaly[/i] · a demon's corruption rating settled back to zero overnight. The demon's BBS handle, when it next posts, is — slightly — not the handle it was.

substrate_anomaly_palette.fingerprint_text: The demon's next post on RUST_CODE has a sign-off it has never used before. The sign-off is a single character: a period.

substrate_anomaly_palette.title: A Graustark node Frasier was sure was held is contested Monday morning
substrate_anomaly_palette.description: The cathedral. The diner. The bungalow. One of the held nodes, on Monday morning, registers as contested without Frasier's network having reported any incident.

engine_effect.log: [i]Anomaly[/i] · one of Graustark's held nodes is, this morning, contested. None of Frasier's people reported anything.

substrate_anomaly_palette.fingerprint_text: John Frank posts on SNACKS: 'Anybody walked past the cathedral this weekend? Door's been unlocked since Saturday. Not by anyone I called.'

substrate_anomaly_palette.title: Agent arrives somewhere they weren't dispatched to
substrate_anomaly_palette.description: Dispatched to Small Wood. Arrived at Harmony Creek. The problem at Harmony Creek is — by accident or by Dean — resolved.

engine_effect.log: [i]Anomaly[/i] · the agent went somewhere else this time. The work, somehow, still got done.

substrate_anomaly_palette.fingerprint_text: No fingerprint. The agent's own BBS thread has, this week, gone quiet on the subject.

substrate_anomaly_palette.title: Escalation clock drifts
substrate_anomaly_palette.description: The region's clock that ticks faster than the spec when Dean is at white. Or slower. Frasier can't tell which way until the day after.

engine_effect.log: [i]Anomaly[/i] · the escalation clock in %s is drifting. Half-speed for the week. Or double. The reading won't settle.

substrate_anomaly_palette.fingerprint_text: A photograph attached to a thread on THE_LIBRARY shows, in the background of an otherwise unrelated post about the public-access channel, lapels being smoothed.

fingerprint_log_palette: On MAINSTREET, a post from a handle nobody recognises: 'the work continues.' Posted at 3:47 AM. Deleted by the sysop at 4 AM.
A photo on THE_ART_WALL has, on the second pass, a figure in the background that wasn't there on the first pass.
Elicia's documentary cuts include, this week, four frames of a man at a counter who wasn't, when she was shooting, at the counter.
John Frank says, on SNACKS, that the regular at the end-stool was the regular at the end-stool except he wasn't. He doesn't elaborate.
Aria's diary on BEDROCK includes the sentence: 'He came in and smoothed his lapels and then he wasn't here.' She is fourteen now. She has not, before, written sentences this declarative.

dean_interlude_seeds.title: The Table 14 Napkin
dean_interlude_seeds.flavor: A single cocktail napkin, folded into thirds, the way men of a certain generation fold them. On the napkin in pencil, in a hand that is the same hand as the hundred-dollar bill: a phone number with the area code crossed out.

dean_interlude_seeds.title: The Tower Postcard
dean_interlude_seeds.flavor: An unstamped postcard, addressed to nobody. Picture side: a tower in Oregon. Text side: 'It is not what it looks like. It has never been what it looks like. The looking is the thing.' No signature.

dean_interlude_seeds.title: The Unread Ledger Page
dean_interlude_seeds.flavor: A single page from Dean's ledger. Three columns: DATE, ACT, BALANCE. The balance column is blank. The acts column lists, in chronological order: 'the cathedral / the boy at the door / the river / her name said aloud / the same / the same / the same / the same.'

dean_interlude_seeds.title: Dean's Ledger
dean_interlude_seeds.flavor:
A ledger page, or a photograph of one, or a memory of a photograph of one — by the fifth fingerprint the distinction has stopped holding. It is ruled in a hand that predates ruling. It is the summer, from the tower.

JUNE 12. New pressure on the river country. Not the county. Not the diocese. Pattern suggests coordination without hierarchy, which the last operator to manage that was — see 1976 file. Watching.

JUNE 30. They spend the small ones on grief work. Extraordinary. The grief was load-bearing; they carry it anyway. Adjusted three outcomes to test elasticity. They absorbed two and never saw the third. Elastic.

JULY 19. The estate is contested now. Whoever schedules their couriers understands the estate better than the developer does. NOTE: the one they call the Vagrant has been here before, under other management. Do not re-acquire. Yet.

AUGUST 2. They noticed the second adjustment. One of them stood in the model home and LOOKED at the seam for eleven minutes. Logged as fingerprint on their side; logged as introduction on mine. We are, at this point, corresponding.

AUGUST 20. Costs rising on both ledgers. Theirs shows in the humans' obligations and the small ones' burn. Mine shows in the brightness, which they read nightly like weather, which is the correct way to read it. They think the tower is the threat. The tower is the OVERHEAD. Filed under: operating expenses, summer, ordinary.

The last line is unentered. The ruling for it is there. The date column, for the last line, is blank the way a held breath is blank — and whoever keeps this ledger rules a line for you before you do the thing, not after.
